title: The Fly
review: I don't think the characters are particularly well established before the telepod action starts. Brundlefly's motivations are desperate and bizarre, as you would expect given the situation, but Brundle's motivations don't make much sense before the accident. Ultimately this doesn't really matter, as the moment the telepods are introdued, you're locked in on what is about to happen. The film is surprisingly blasé about the philosophical implications of being broken down and reconstructed, although in fairness I like how focussed it is on the genetic mixing. It might have ended up much longer and less cohesive if Brundlefly was also grappling with whether Brundle experienced death or continuation during the teleportation. I think it's insane how he used a live baboon to test the teleportation of organic matter every time, and he only later realised it would be easier to use a small amount of raw meat. An excellent film, with all the effects and horror you want from Cronenberg, and a more accessible and explicit plot than some of his other works.
